content:
💡 SmartImage | C#
🎯 Primary Use Case: Finding the original source or similar instances of an image by leveraging a wide array of reverse image search engines.
✨ Key Features: • Supports multiple reverse image search engines (SauceNao, IQDB, Ascii2D, trace.moe, Google Images, TinEye, Yandex, Bing, etc.) • Provides both a Windows GUI and a cross-platform Rdx command-line tool • Allows searching via direct image links or files • Configurable search behavior and output • Context menu integration for Windows GUI
📖 Summary: SmartImage is a C# application that performs reverse image searches across multiple engines. It offers both a Windows GUI and a cross-platform command-line interface (Rdx) to help users find the source of an image.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 yj_nearbyglasses | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: To alert users to the potential presence of nearby smart glasses, serving as a privacy-protection tool.
✨ Key Features: • Detects nearby smart glasses via Bluetooth signals • Warns the user of potential smart glasses presence • Free and open-source • No telemetry or data collection from the app itself • Local log file export (user-controlled)
📖 Summary: This Android application, written in Kotlin, aims to detect nearby smart glasses by analyzing their Bluetooth signals and alerting the user. It focuses on privacy by not collecting user data, but warns of potential false positives and advises extreme caution in interpreting its alerts.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 paneru | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a flexible and efficient window management experience on macOS, particularly for users who prefer tiling but find traditional implementations suboptimal on large or small screens.
✨ Key Features: • Sliding, tiling window management on an infinite strip • Independent window strips per monitor • No resizing of existing windows when new ones open • Works with macOS workspaces and separate spaces • Focus follows mouse
📖 Summary: Paneru is a sliding, tiling window manager for macOS that arranges windows on an infinite strip, ensuring existing layouts are never resized when new windows are opened. It operates independently on each monitor and is designed to optimize usability for both large and small displays.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 desk_troll | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: A prank or gag application to temporarily disrupt a user's Windows desktop experience.
✨ Key Features: • Animated characters running on the desktop • Simulated fake desktop using a screenshot • Interactive elements like 'stealing' icons • Inverted mouse control • Clicking erases parts of the fake desktop
📖 Summary: This repository contains a prank application for Windows called 'desk_troll'. Upon execution, it simulates a fake desktop by displaying a screenshot of the user's actual desktop, while animated characters (a penguin and a fly) interact with it, 'stealing' icons and making the mouse behave erratically. The application is designed to be difficult to close, requiring a specific password.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 OnlyHuman | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Filtering out AI-generated SEO spam and low-effort content from web search results and feeds.
✨ Key Features: • Blocks AI-SEO content farms • Blocks thin SEO sites • Blocks spun content and rewrites • Blocks search result spam • Integrates with uBlock Origin
📖 Summary: OnlyHuman is a filter list for uBlock Origin designed to block AI-generated SEO spam, thin content websites, and other low-effort content from search results and feeds. It aims to improve the quality of online information by removing junk content that exploits search engines.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 lmms | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Digital audio workstation for music production
✨ Key Features: • Song-Editor for arranging melodies, samples, patterns, and automation • Pattern-Editor for creating beats and patterns • Piano-Roll for editing patterns and melodies • Mixer with unlimited mixer channels and arbitrary number of effects • Instrument and effect-plugins
📖 Summary: LMMS is a free, open-source, cross-platform digital audio workstation for music production. It provides a comprehensive suite of tools for composing, arranging, and mixing music, including a song editor, piano roll, beat sequencer, and mixer.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
